eod.conclusion: Markets closed higher for the third straight session despite losing some early gains. The advance came amid reduced volatility and ahead of key earnings reports, with banking stocks leading the charge. While indices showed resilience, currency weakness and sector rotations suggested underlying caution as investors awaited corporate results to guide future moves.

premarket.market_expectations: Neutral bias expected with mixed signals. S&P futures dipped slightly while Nasdaq fell more sharply. Bond yields and dollar weakened, but domestic institutional buying provided support. Rising oil prices and rupee depreciation add pressure. Previous close near the high end of its range suggests some upward momentum remains.
